욥기 41:1.content: “Can you draw out Leviathan with a fish hook, or press down his tongue with a cord?

욥기 41:2.content: Can you put a rope into his nose, or pierce his jaw through with a hook?

욥기 41:3.content: Will he make many petitions to you, or will he speak soft words to you?

욥기 41:4.content: Will he make a covenant with you, that you should take him for a servant forever?

욥기 41:5.content: Will you play with him as with a bird? Or will you bind him for your girls?

욥기 41:6.content: Will traders barter for him? Will they part him among the merchants?

욥기 41:7.content: Can you fill his skin with barbed irons, or his head with fish spears?

욥기 41:8.content: Lay your hand on him. Remember the battle, and do so no more.

욥기 41:9.content: Behold, the hope of him is in vain. Won’t one be cast down even at the sight of him?

욥기 41:10.content: None is so fierce that he dare stir him up. Who then is he who can stand before me?

욥기 41:11.content: Who has first given to me, that I should repay him? Everything under the heavens is mine.

욥기 41:12.content: “I will not keep silence concerning his limbs, nor his mighty strength, nor his goodly frame.

욥기 41:13.content: Who can strip off his outer garment? Who will come within his jaws?

욥기 41:14.content: Who can open the doors of his face? Around his teeth is terror.

욥기 41:15.content: Strong scales are his pride, shut up together with a close seal.

욥기 41:16.content: One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.

욥기 41:17.content: They are joined to one another. They stick together, so that they can’t be pulled apart.

욥기 41:18.content: His sneezing flashes out light. His eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.

욥기 41:19.content: Out of his mouth go burning torches. Sparks of fire leap out.

욥기 41:20.content: Out of his nostrils a smoke goes, as of a boiling pot over a fire of reeds.

욥기 41:21.content: His breath kindles coals. A flame goes out of his mouth.

욥기 41:22.content: There is strength in his neck. Terror dances before him.

욥기 41:23.content: The flakes of his flesh are joined together. They are firm on him. They can’t be moved.

욥기 41:24.content: His heart is as firm as a stone, yes, firm as the lower millstone.

욥기 41:25.content: When he raises himself up, the mighty are afraid. They retreat before his thrashing.

욥기 41:26.content: If one attacks him with the sword, it can’t prevail; nor the spear, the dart, nor the pointed shaft.

욥기 41:27.content: He counts iron as straw; and bronze as rotten wood.

욥기 41:28.content: The arrow can’t make him flee. Sling stones are like chaff to him.

욥기 41:29.content: Clubs are counted as stubble. He laughs at the rushing of the javelin.

욥기 41:30.content: His undersides are like sharp potsherds, leaving a trail in the mud like a threshing sledge.

욥기 41:31.content: He makes the deep to boil like a pot. He makes the sea like a pot of ointment.

욥기 41:32.content: He makes a path shine after him. One would think the deep had white hair.

욥기 41:33.content: On earth there is not his equal, that is made without fear.

욥기 41:34.content: He sees everything that is high. He is king over all the sons of pride.”
